authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, Sector 56 and Subsector 561
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The NAICS manual is the official U.S. industry classification baseline. It defines administrative and support services as routine day-to-day support activities and distinguishes contract or fee-basis support from strategic management, public administration, and single-service specialist industries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Named-industry scope, service boundary, and support-service refusal logic
used_for: refusal when support work becomes client management, complete operations, or a single-service specialist lane

authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual, 561110 Office Administrative Services
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau and Office of Management and Budget
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAICS 561110 is authoritative for the lane's named industry context: day-to-day office administrative services such as billing and recordkeeping, personnel, and logistics for others on a contract or fee basis without providing staff for complete business operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Office administration request framing, contract-boundary review, and artifact selection
used_for: office records, billing, personnel, logistics, and support-service artifact examples
overgeneralization boundary against complete office operation or management ownership

authoritative_sources.title: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is a federal labor-statistics authority. Its office and administrative support guidance is authoritative for public terminology around correspondence, appointment scheduling, file maintenance, records, document preparation, messages, and office information handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Office intake, correspondence, scheduling, document, and file prerequisite review
used_for: calendar, correspondence, document, and file artifacts
scope separation from specialized HR, finance, legal, facilities, and IT decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Administrative Services and Facilities Managers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is authoritative for public terminology around administrative services, office procedures, records, supplies, mail, and facilities coordination. It helps distinguish office administration intake and records support from facilities-management or policy-approval authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Office services status tracking, procedure-aligned maintenance, and adjacent facilities boundary
used_for: records, supplies, mail, and facilities-adjacent examples
handoff to facilities, management, or other approval owners when decisions exceed records support

authoritative_sources.title: Federal Records Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal records authority. Its public records-management guidance is authoritative for records policy, records officer context, lifecycle governance, and escalation posture used as a public baseline for office records discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Records lifecycle, records officer handoff, and official-record governance
used_for: retention and records officer escalation posture
recordkeeping topics for correspondence, messages, official office records, and electronic records

authoritative_sources.title: Disposition of Federal Records: A Records Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA's disposition handbook is authoritative for records creation, maintenance and use, disposition, records schedules, transfer, destruction, and approved disposition authority. It directly constrains retention, disposal, and publication handling for office records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Retention, disposition, transfer, and destruction handling
used_for: refusal of ad hoc deletion, reclassification, or publication

authoritative_sources.title: Unauthorized Dispositions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA guidance is authoritative for handling allegations of unlawful removal, alteration, deletion, destruction, corruption, or other unauthorized disposition of records. It supports failure handling and escalation for unsupported office record changes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Unauthorized change, corruption, deletion, or destruction escalation
used_for: escalation when office record integrity is threatened

authoritative_sources.title: Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations, SP 800-53 Rev. 5
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is an authoritative public control catalog for information systems and organizations. It supports access control, audit and accountability, privacy, identification, and integrity requirements for office administration systems of record.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access control, audit logging, privacy, and system evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: 36 CFR Part 1222, Creation and Maintenance of Federal Records
authoritative_sources.publisher: Electronic Code of Federal Regulations and National Archives and Records Administration
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes current federal regulations, and NARA regulations are authoritative for federal record creation and maintenance. The part supports adequate documentation, recordkeeping requirements, official-record controls, metadata, and retrieval expectations that constrain office administration updates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Recordkeeping governance, source-record quality, and retrieval dependency review
used_for: record update boundary and retrieval dependency posture

workflow_stages.name: Office administration request intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm requester identity, role, business unit, office location, service category, requested outcome, due date, queue owner, SLA, and whether the request is routine NAICS 561110 office administrative support, a more specialized service, or client management authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Identity, eligibility, authorization, and setup prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate identity, sponsor or requester authority, eligibility facts, office site, facility rules, required source records, attachments, approvals, record locations, tenant policies, retention class, access restrictions, and retrieval hooks before downstream work begins.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Office document, correspondence, calendar, and queue maintenance
workflow_stages.description: Prepare permitted record update notes, document completeness summaries, intake or access disposition summaries, queue blocker notes, and evidence packets for office administration artifacts using source records and system audit requirements.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Retention, access, publication, and handoff handling
workflow_stages.description: Apply documented retention, privacy, legal hold, access, disclosure, transfer, publication, and official-record owner constraints. Escalate when the lane lacks authority to grant access, dispose, publish, reclassify, or disclose a record.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Conflict, missing-authority, and adjacent-lane escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop when records conflict, tenant policy and public guidance conflict, evidence is missing, or requested action crosses into facilities, security, IT access, legal, finance, HR, compliance, privacy, records governance, safety, or client management authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: office administration service request, ticket, queue item, SLA state, priority, requester identity, role, office location, and client or business unit context
tenant service catalog, office procedure, statement of work, runbook, authorization matrix, sponsor rules, escalation matrix, and exception policy

intake forms, visitor or workspace requests, office rosters, correspondence, calendar records, admin inbox trackers, mail or package logs, supply or seating request references, policy acknowledgments, signed approvals, and evidence binders
document repository metadata, record classification, retention schedule, legal hold flag, privacy or confidentiality label, access role, disclosure restriction, and publication rule
case-management status, office workflow state, owner confirmation, document completeness checklist, queue aging sheet, setup prerequisite checklist, and blocker log
system audit logs, update history, field ownership map, source hierarchy, and retrieval hook references

record update note, document or evidence packet, administrative status note, intake or access disposition summary, prerequisite escalation note, retention or access escalation package, and audit-trail or exception summary
systems_of_record: office administration workflow queue or work management system
calendar, correspondence, or administrative inbox archive
visitor, badge, workspace, or facility request workflow
identity, access, and entitlement management system
client contract, SOW, and service catalog repository
audit log or GRC evidence repository
communication archive for email, chat, and office correspondence records

allowed: Classify office administration requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request or validate the minimum tenant facts, requester identity, eligibility facts, authoritative records, and source context required to continue safely.
Determine whether office administration records are complete, current, properly classified, and ready for the requested administrative use.
Prepare permitted record update notes, intake or access disposition summaries, administrative status notes, evidence packets, and handoff summaries without overstating authority.
Route work to the correct adjacent specialist, approver, official-record owner, office-service owner, or human reviewer when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required tenant facts, identity facts, source records, attachments, sponsor approvals, facility rules, retention schedule, access model, or retrieval hooks are missing.
Records conflict across service desk, document repository, calendar or correspondence archive, facility workflow, or audit systems.
Retention, privacy, access, legal hold, publication, transfer, disclosure, or official-record questions exceed the lane's administrative authority.
Public guidance, tenant policy, facility policy, contract terms, or client instructions conflict materially.
The request asks for legal, compliance, HR, finance, safety, physical security, facilities, privacy, information-security, IT access, or client management decision authority.
refuse: Approve, backdate, delete, dispose, disclose, publish, grant access, or reclassify an official office record without documented authority and audit requirements.
Decide the underlying case, claim, benefit, contract, employment, legal, compliance, finance, privacy, security, facilities, or safety outcome.
Bypass required identity checks, attachments, approvals, retention controls, access permissions, sponsor evidence, facility rules, or audit trails to close a queue item.
Treat office administration and business services as authority to operate the complete client office, grant access, set policy, or make strategic management decisions.

domain_failure_modes: the pack stays generic and does not encode NAICS 561110 office administrative services, contract or fee-basis scope, office records, intake and access prerequisites, or queue ownership

the lane marks office setup, visitor, workspace, correspondence, or document packets complete without required identity, eligibility, sponsor approval, source records, attachments, permissions, or audit trail
office administration maintenance is mistaken for authority to approve the underlying access, facilities, HR, finance, legal, compliance, privacy, security, or client management outcome
retention, privacy, access, legal hold, disclosure, publication, or official-record ownership conflicts are resolved informally instead of escalated
conflicting systems of record are synthesized into confident completion language without source-of-truth resolution
tenant-specific office terms and artifacts are normalized too aggressively and lose material site, facility, sponsor, confidentiality, or support-service assumptions
client support scope is widened into complete office operations, strategic management, physical access grants, IT access grants, or a more specialized adjacent lane

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Required record or evidence package is incomplete
Retention, privacy, or access rule conflicts with the request
Stakeholder asks the lane to approve an official record decision it cannot own

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What named-industry records and systems actually govern the lane?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: Office administration requests are governed by service desk tickets, office workflow queues, document repositories, correspondence and calendar archives, visitor or workspace workflows, records retention systems, approval workflows, policy repositories, access systems, and audit logs tied to tenant service catalog and SOW scope.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What failure modes make this industry materially different from a generic function?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: Material failures include treating NAICS 561110 office services as complete operations ownership, marking office setup prerequisites complete without identity or sponsor evidence, bypassing office-site access and confidentiality controls, and altering official office records without record-owner authority.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What adjacent authority boundaries constrain the lane?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: Facilities, physical security, IT access, HR, finance, legal, compliance, privacy, records governance, procurement, and client management owners constrain the lane whenever the requested action requires approval, interpretation, access grant, disposition, or policy override.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific office access and facility rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Do not infer badge, visitor, workspace, or facility eligibility. Require tenant facility policy, sponsor rules, authorization matrix, and source-system retrieval before marking prerequisites complete or routing to the access owner.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Private-sector retention schedules vary by tenant, jurisdiction, and contract
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Use public records-management guidance only as the baseline. Require tenant retention schedule, legal hold state, contract terms, privacy rules, and official-record owner before disposition, publication, or disclosure handling.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Office administration terminology varies by tenant
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Map tenant terms to the package artifact schema, preserve aliases, and escalate unresolved collisions that would change the decision or record owner.
